answer:
In Mirza Ghalib's *Diwan-e-Ghalib*, the Urdu poetic term *naqsh* (literally "imprint," "trace," "mark," or "design") frequently carries a layered, symbolic resonance beyond its denotative senses of physical mark or image. It often evokes the **fleeting, transformative trace of the beloved's presence**—most iconically as *naqsh-e-qadam* ("footprint" or "footstep's trace")—imbuing mundane paths or landscapes with paradisiacal beauty, longing, or spiritual ecstasy amid love's torment.[^1][^2] This motif underscores themes of absence, desire, and illusion: the lover discerns the beloved's *naqsh* where none fully persists, mirroring the heart's obsessive projection.[^3]

It also appears in compounds like *naqsh-e-wafa* ("imprint/mark of fidelity"), lamenting loyalty's failure to endure or remedy worldly pain.[^4] Less commonly, it suggests an ephemeral "design" or "image" of the self, akin to a vanishing footprint in sand or wave.[^5][^6]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 90 ("Jahan tera naqsh-e-qadam dekhte hain"), [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/90-jahan-tera-naqsh-e-qadam-dekhte-hain/translation/#p-b88355).
[^2]: Ibid., [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/90-jahan-tera-naqsh-e-qadam-dekhte-hain/translation/).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 6 ("Aamad-e-khat se hua hai sard jo bazaar-e-dost"), [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/06-aamad-e-khat-se-hua-hai-sard-jo-bazaar-e-dost/translation/#p-6f525a).
[^4]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 29 ("Dehr mein naqsh-e-wafa wajah-e-tasalli na hua"), [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/29-dehr-mein-naqsh-e-wafa-wajah-e-tasalli-na-hua/translation/#p-cffc40).
[^5]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 145 ("Na hoga yak-biyabaan maandagi se zauq kam mera"), [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/145-na-hoga-yak-biyabaan-maandagi-se-zauq-kam-mera/translation/#p-070a9e).
[^6]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 7 ("Aamad-e-sailaab-e-toofan-e-sada-e-aab hai"), [translation/](works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/07-aamad-e-sailaab-e-toofan-e-sada-e-aab-hai/translation/#p-e53fdb).
